test_cases.description: Verifies that the config file at $HOME/.af/config.toml is a valid TOML file containing all four required fields: hub_url, user_id, api_key, and key_id.
preconditions: $HOME/.af/config.toml exists and was previously written by a config-mutating command
Go test binary can read $HOME/.af/config.toml
test_cases.input: A config struct with hub_url='https://hub.example.com', user_id='uid-123', api_key='key-abc', key_id='kid-xyz'
test_cases.expected: The file at $HOME/.af/config.toml is parseable as TOML and all four fields decode to their expected values
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: writeConfig(cfg); contents = readFile('$HOME/.af/config.toml'); decoded = toml.Decode(contents); assert decoded.hub_url == 'https://hub.example.com'; assert decoded.user_id == 'uid-123'; assert decoded.api_key == 'key-abc'; assert decoded.key_id == 'kid-xyz'

test_cases.description: Verifies that invoking any CLI command when $HOME/.af/config.toml does not exist creates the directory with mode 0700 and the file with mode 0600 containing empty field values.
preconditions: $HOME/.af/ directory does not exist
Test uses a temporary directory as $HOME
test_cases.input: afc invoked with any subcommand in an environment where $HOME points to a temp dir with no .af/ subdirectory
test_cases.expected: Directory $HOME/.af/ is created with mode 0700; file $HOME/.af/config.toml is created with mode 0600; file contains valid TOML with empty hub_url, user_id, api_key, key_id
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: tmpHome = createTempDir(); setEnv('HOME', tmpHome); runCLI(['keys', 'list']); dirStat = stat(tmpHome + '/.af'); assert dirStat.Mode().Perm() == 0700; fileStat = stat(tmpHome + '/.af/config.toml'); assert fileStat.Mode().Perm() == 0600; contents = readFile(tmpHome + '/.af/config.toml'); decoded = toml.Decode(contents); assert decoded.hub_url == ''

test_cases.description: Verifies that a malformed (unparseable TOML) config file causes the CLI to print a descriptive error to stderr and exit with code 1 without modifying the file.
preconditions: $HOME/.af/config.toml exists with invalid TOML content (e.g., 'not = [valid toml')
Test uses a temporary directory as $HOME
test_cases.input: Config file contents: 'not = [valid toml'
test_cases.expected: CLI exits with code 1; stderr contains 'Error: failed to parse config file:'; config file remains unchanged with the original invalid content
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: writeFile(configPath, 'not = [valid toml'); result = runCLI(['keys', 'list']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'Error: failed to parse config file:'; assert readFile(configPath) == 'not = [valid toml'

test_cases.description: Verifies that config resolution precedence is flag > env var > config file for hub_url, user_id, and api_key.
preconditions: Config file contains hub_url='file-url', user_id='file-user', api_key='file-key'
AF_HUB_URL env var is set to 'env-url'
--hub-url flag is set to 'flag-url'
test_cases.input: All three sources provide different hub_url values; env var provides user_id; config file provides api_key
test_cases.expected: Resolved hub_url equals 'flag-url'; resolved user_id equals 'env-user' (env var overrides config); resolved api_key equals 'file-key' (config used when no flag or env var)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: writeConfig({hub_url:'file-url', user_id:'file-user', api_key:'file-key'}); setEnv('AF_HUB_URL','env-url'); setEnv('AF_HUB_USER_ID','env-user'); cfg = resolveConfig(['--hub-url','flag-url']); assert cfg.hub_url == 'flag-url'; assert cfg.user_id == 'env-user'; assert cfg.api_key == 'file-key'

test_cases.description: Verifies that key_id is resolved exclusively from the config file and has no CLI flag or environment variable override.
preconditions: Config file contains key_id='kid-from-file'
No CLI flag or env var named key_id exists
test_cases.input: Config file with key_id='kid-from-file'; attempt to set key_id via env var (should be ignored)
test_cases.expected: Resolved key_id equals 'kid-from-file' from the config file; no --key-id flag exists in the CLI
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: writeConfig({key_id:'kid-from-file'}); cfg = resolveConfig([]); assert cfg.key_id == 'kid-from-file'; assert CLI.hasFlag('--key-id') == false

test_cases.description: Verifies that when a required config value is missing from all sources, the CLI prints a descriptive error naming the missing value and exits with code 1.
preconditions: Config file is empty (no hub_url, user_id, api_key)
No relevant env vars set
No CLI flags provided for hub_url
test_cases.input: Command requiring hub_url invoked with no flag, no env var, no config file value
test_cases.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains a message naming hub_url as missing and explaining how to set it (via --hub-url flag, AF_HUB_URL env var, or config file)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: writeConfig({}); clearEnv('AF_HUB_URL','AF_HUB_USER_ID','AF_HUB_API_KEY'); result = runCLI(['workspace','list']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'hub_url'; assert result.stderr contains '--hub-url' OR result.stderr contains 'AF_HUB_URL'

test_cases.description: Verifies that config mutation creates a temp file in $HOME/.af/ with os.CreateTemp and then atomically renames it to config.toml, with correct final content.
preconditions: Existing $HOME/.af/config.toml with known content
Filesystem allows rename in the .af directory
test_cases.input: New config values: hub_url='https://new.example.com', user_id='new-uid', api_key='new-key', key_id='new-kid'
test_cases.expected: Temp file created in $HOME/.af/ with prefix 'config.toml.'; temp file renamed to config.toml; final config.toml contains the new values; no temp file remains after successful write
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: oldConfig = {hub_url:'old', ...}; writeConfig(oldConfig); observedFiles = []; hookCreateTemp(fn(name){ observedFiles.append(name) }); writeConfigAtomic({hub_url:'https://new.example.com', user_id:'new-uid', api_key:'new-key', key_id:'new-kid'}); assert any(f starts_with '$HOME/.af/config.toml.' for f in observedFiles); assert readParsedConfig().hub_url == 'https://new.example.com'; assert not exists(tempFile)

test_cases.description: Verifies that the temporary config file and the final config.toml both have mode 0600.
preconditions: $HOME/.af/ directory exists with mode 0700
Filesystem supports POSIX file permissions
test_cases.input: Config mutation with any valid config values
test_cases.expected: Temp file has mode 0600; after rename, config.toml has mode 0600
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: tmpPath = capturedTempFilePath; writeConfigAtomic(cfg); assert stat(tmpPath).Mode().Perm() == 0600 OR assert stat(configPath).Mode().Perm() == 0600; assert stat(configPath).Mode().Perm() == 0600

test_cases.description: Verifies that invoking afc with no subcommand prints help text to stdout and exits with code 0.
preconditions: afc binary is built and available
test_cases.input: afc invoked with no arguments
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; stdout contains help text (command name, usage, and list of subcommands); stderr is empty
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: result = runCLI([]); assert result.exitCode == 0; assert result.stdout contains 'Usage:'; assert result.stdout contains 'login'; assert result.stdout contains 'workspace'

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc --version prints 'afc version <semver>' to stdout and exits with code 0.
preconditions: afc binary compiled with -ldflags injecting version='0.1.0'
test_cases.input: afc --version
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; stdout contains 'afc version 0.1.0'; stderr is empty
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: result = runCLI(['--version']); assert result.exitCode == 0; assert result.stdout contains 'afc version 0.1.0'

test_cases.description: Verifies that --help and 'afc help <command>' are supported for all commands and produce help text on stdout with exit code 0.
preconditions: afc binary is built and available
test_cases.input: afc --help and afc help login
test_cases.expected: Both invocations exit with code 0; stdout contains command description and flags for the respective command
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: r1 = runCLI(['--help']); assert r1.exitCode == 0; assert r1.stdout contains 'Usage:'; r2 = runCLI(['help','login']); assert r2.exitCode == 0; assert r2.stdout contains 'login'; assert r2.stdout contains '--provider'

test_cases.description: Verifies that --expires with a value not in {0,30,60,90} causes the CLI to print the exact error message to stderr and exit with code 1 before any network request.
preconditions: Mock HTTP server is not started (no network calls expected)
Valid config file with hub_url, api_key
test_cases.input: afc login --provider github --expires 45
test_cases.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains exactly 'Error: --expires must be one of: 0, 30, 60, 90'; no HTTP request made
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: networkCalls = []; result = runCLI(['login','--provider','github','--expires','45'], mockHTTP(networkCalls)); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'Error: --expires must be one of: 0, 30, 60, 90'; assert len(networkCalls) == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that providing --git-url as an empty string for workspace create causes the CLI to print a descriptive error and exit with code 1 before any network request.
preconditions: Valid config file with hub_url, api_key
No mock HTTP server (no network calls expected)
test_cases.input: afc workspace create --git-url '' --slug my-ws
test_cases.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains a descriptive error about --git-url being empty; no HTTP request made
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: networkCalls = []; result = runCLI(['workspace','create','--git-url','','--slug','my-ws'], mockHTTP(networkCalls)); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains '--git-url' OR result.stderr contains 'git-url'; assert len(networkCalls) == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that providing --workspace as an empty string for workspace token commands causes the CLI to print a descriptive error and exit with code 1 before any network request.
preconditions: Valid config file with hub_url, api_key
No mock HTTP server (no network calls expected)
test_cases.input: afc workspace token list --workspace ''
test_cases.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains a descriptive error about --workspace being empty; no HTTP request made
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: networkCalls = []; result = runCLI(['workspace','token','list','--workspace',''], mockHTTP(networkCalls)); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains '--workspace' OR result.stderr contains 'workspace'; assert len(networkCalls) == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc login fetches providers, validates the --provider flag, generates a CSRF state, starts a callback server, and opens the authorization URL in the browser.
preconditions: Mock HTTP server handles GET /api/v1/auth/providers returning ['github']
browser.OpenURL is replaced with a test stub that captures the URL
Config file exists with hub_url pointing to mock server
test_cases.input: afc login --provider github --expires 90
test_cases.expected: GET /api/v1/auth/providers is called; CSRF state parameter is non-empty with >=16 bytes entropy; local HTTP server starts and listens on a random port; authorization URL is passed to the browser stub; authorization URL is printed to stderr
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer = startMockServer(GET '/api/v1/auth/providers' => 200, ['github']); browserURLs = []; stubBrowserOpen(browserURLs); result = runLoginAsync(['--provider','github','--expires','90']); assert mockServer.received('GET','/api/v1/auth/providers'); assert len(browserURLs) == 1; authURL = browserURLs[0]; parsedURL = parseURL(authURL); state = parsedURL.queryParam('state'); assert len(base64Decode(state)) >= 16; assert parsedURL.queryParam('redirect_uri') matches 'http://localhost:\d+/callback'; assert result.stderr contains authURL

test_cases.description: Verifies that the full authorization URL is always printed to stderr regardless of whether the browser opened successfully.
preconditions: Mock HTTP server returns providers list with 'github'
browser.OpenURL stub returns an error (simulating browser failure)
Config file with hub_url pointing to mock server
test_cases.input: afc login --provider github; browser.OpenURL returns error
test_cases.expected: Authorization URL appears on stderr even when browser open fails; login flow continues waiting for callback
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: stubBrowserOpenError(error('browser not found')); result = runLoginAsync(['--provider','github']); assert result.stderr contains 'http://localhost:'; assert result.stderr contains '/callback'

test_cases.description: Verifies that upon receiving the OAuth callback the CLI validates the state, captures the code, responds with HTTP 200 HTML success page, and exchanges the code with the hub.
preconditions: Mock HTTP server handles GET /api/v1/auth/providers returning ['github']
Mock HTTP server handles POST /api/v1/auth/callback returning valid user and api_key JSON
Config file with hub_url pointing to mock server
Login flow is in progress waiting for callback
test_cases.input: Browser redirect to callback URL with correct state and code='test-code'
test_cases.expected: Callback receives HTTP 200 with HTML body containing 'Login successful!'; POST /api/v1/auth/callback is called with code='test-code'; flow completes successfully
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: state = capturedStateFromAuthURL; cbResp = httpGet('http://localhost:'+port+'/callback?code=test-code&state='+state); assert cbResp.statusCode == 200; assert cbResp.body contains 'Login successful'; assert cbResp.contentType contains 'text/html'; assert mockServer.received('POST','/api/v1/auth/callback') with body.code == 'test-code'

test_cases.description: Verifies that upon successful POST /api/v1/auth/callback the CLI writes hub_url, user_id, api_key, and key_id to config via atomic write and exits with code 0.
preconditions: Mock server returns {user:{id:'u-1'}, api_key:{id:'k-1', key:'secret'}} for POST /api/v1/auth/callback
Config file with hub_url; config initially has no api_key or user_id
Callback is triggered with valid state and code
test_cases.input: Successful OAuth callback exchange with --provider github --expires 90
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; config file updated with hub_url, user_id='u-1', api_key='secret', key_id='k-1'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: triggerSuccessfulLogin(provider='github', expires=90, serverResp={user:{id:'u-1'},api_key:{id:'k-1',key:'secret'}}); assert exitCode == 0; cfg = readParsedConfig(); assert cfg.user_id == 'u-1'; assert cfg.api_key == 'secret'; assert cfg.key_id == 'k-1'; assert cfg.hub_url == mockServerURL

test_cases.description: Verifies that the POST /api/v1/auth/callback payload contains provider, code, redirect_uri, and expires as an integer.
preconditions: Mock server captures the request body for POST /api/v1/auth/callback
Login callback triggered with code='abc' and --expires 0
test_cases.input: afc login --provider github --expires 0; callback received
test_cases.expected: Request body JSON contains provider='github', code='abc', redirect_uri='http://localhost:<port>/callback', expires=0 (integer not string)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: capturedBody = mockServer.lastRequestBody('POST','/api/v1/auth/callback'); parsed = jsonParse(capturedBody); assert parsed.provider == 'github'; assert parsed.code == 'abc'; assert parsed.redirect_uri matches 'http://localhost:\d+/callback'; assert typeof(parsed.expires) == integer; assert parsed.expires == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc login silently overwrites existing credentials in config when api_key is already set, without revoking the old key.
preconditions: Config file contains hub_url, user_id='old-uid', api_key='old-key', key_id='old-kid'
Mock server returns new credentials {user:{id:'new-uid'}, api_key:{id:'new-kid', key:'new-key'}}
Mock server does NOT receive a DELETE /api/v1/keys/ request
test_cases.input: afc login --provider github with existing credentials in config
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; config updated with new credentials; DELETE /api/v1/keys/ never called; old key remains on server
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: writeConfig({api_key:'old-key',key_id:'old-kid',user_id:'old-uid'}); triggerSuccessfulLogin(serverResp={user:{id:'new-uid'},api_key:{id:'new-kid',key:'new-key'}}); cfg = readParsedConfig(); assert cfg.api_key == 'new-key'; assert cfg.key_id == 'new-kid'; assert cfg.user_id == 'new-uid'; assert mockServer.notReceived('DELETE','/api/v1/keys/')

test_cases.description: Verifies that when --provider value is not in the providers list, the CLI prints the exact error message and exits with code 1 without initiating any OAuth flow.
preconditions: Mock server returns ['github'] for GET /api/v1/auth/providers
No browser is opened
test_cases.input: afc login --provider gitlab
test_cases.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains 'Error: unsupported provider: gitlab. Available: github'; no browser opened; no callback server started
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: browserOpened = false; stubBrowserOpen(fn(url){ browserOpened = true }); result = runCLI(['login','--provider','gitlab']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'Error: unsupported provider: gitlab. Available: github'; assert browserOpened == false

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc keys list sends GET /api/v1/keys with Bearer auth header and prints the pretty-printed JSON response body to stdout.
preconditions: Mock server returns JSON array of keys for GET /api/v1/keys
Config file has hub_url, api_key='test-key'
test_cases.input: afc keys list
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; GET /api/v1/keys called with Authorization: Bearer test-key; stdout contains pretty-printed (2-space indent) JSON array; stderr is empty
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
mockServer.handle('GET','/api/v1/keys', 200, [{id:'k1'}]); result = runCLI(['keys','list']); assert result.exitCode == 0; req = mockServer.lastRequest('GET','/api/v1/keys'); assert req.headers['Authorization'] == 'Bearer test-key'; parsed = jsonParse(result.stdout); assert parsed[0].id == 'k1'; assert result.stdout matches /\{
  / (2-space indent)

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc keys refresh sends POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh, updates api_key and key_id in config, and prints the response JSON to stdout.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key='old-key', key_id='old-kid'
Mock server handles POST /api/v1/keys/old-kid/refresh returning {id:'new-kid', key:'new-key'}
test_cases.input: afc keys refresh
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; POST /api/v1/keys/old-kid/refresh called with Bearer auth; config updated with api_key='new-key', key_id='new-kid'; stdout contains pretty-printed response JSON
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: writeConfig({api_key:'old-key',key_id:'old-kid'}); mockServer.handle('POST','/api/v1/keys/old-kid/refresh', 200, {id:'new-kid',key:'new-key'}); result = runCLI(['keys','refresh']); assert result.exitCode == 0; cfg = readParsedConfig(); assert cfg.api_key == 'new-key'; assert cfg.key_id == 'new-kid'; parsed = jsonParse(result.stdout); assert parsed.id == 'new-kid'

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc keys refresh with missing key_id in config prints the exact error message and exits with code 1 without any network request.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url and api_key but key_id is absent or empty
test_cases.input: afc keys refresh with config missing key_id
test_cases.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains 'Error: key_id is not set. Run "afc login" first.'; no network request made
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: writeConfig({hub_url:'http://hub.example.com',api_key:'k',key_id:''}); networkCalls=[]; result = runCLI(['keys','refresh'], mockHTTP(networkCalls)); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'Error: key_id is not set. Run "afc login" first.'; assert len(networkCalls) == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc keys revoke on 2xx response clears api_key, key_id, user_id from config and prints 'API key revoked.' to stderr.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key='my-key', key_id='my-kid', user_id='my-uid'
Mock server returns 204 for DELETE /api/v1/keys/my-kid
test_cases.input: afc keys revoke
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; config api_key, key_id, user_id are empty strings; stderr contains 'API key revoked.'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: writeConfig({api_key:'my-key',key_id:'my-kid',user_id:'my-uid'}); mockServer.handle('DELETE','/api/v1/keys/my-kid', 204, ''); result = runCLI(['keys','revoke']); assert result.exitCode == 0; cfg = readParsedConfig(); assert cfg.api_key == ''; assert cfg.key_id == ''; assert cfg.user_id == ''; assert result.stderr contains 'API key revoked.'

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc keys revoke on 404 response clears local credentials and prints 'API key not found on server. Local credentials cleared.' to stderr.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key='gone-key', key_id='gone-kid', user_id='gone-uid'
Mock server returns 404 for DELETE /api/v1/keys/gone-kid
test_cases.input: afc keys revoke
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; config api_key, key_id, user_id cleared; stderr contains 'API key not found on server. Local credentials cleared.'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: writeConfig({api_key:'gone-key',key_id:'gone-kid',user_id:'gone-uid'}); mockServer.handle('DELETE','/api/v1/keys/gone-kid', 404, {error:'not found'}); result = runCLI(['keys','revoke']); assert result.exitCode == 0; cfg = readParsedConfig(); assert cfg.api_key == ''; assert cfg.key_id == ''; assert cfg.user_id == ''; assert result.stderr contains 'API key not found on server. Local credentials cleared.'

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc keys revoke with missing key_id prints the exact error message and exits with code 1 without any network request.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url and api_key but key_id is absent or empty
test_cases.input: afc keys revoke with config missing key_id
test_cases.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains 'Error: key_id is not set. Run "afc login" first.'; no network request made
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: writeConfig({hub_url:'http://hub.example.com',api_key:'k',key_id:''}); networkCalls=[]; result = runCLI(['keys','revoke'], mockHTTP(networkCalls)); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'Error: key_id is not set. Run "afc login" first.'; assert len(networkCalls) == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc keys revoke on a non-2xx non-404 response prints the error to stderr, exits with code 1, and does not modify config.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key='k', key_id='kid', user_id='uid'
Mock server returns 500 with JSON error body for DELETE /api/v1/keys/kid
test_cases.input: afc keys revoke; server returns 500 {error: 'internal server error'}
test_cases.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains the error message from JSON body; config unchanged (api_key, key_id, user_id still present)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: writeConfig({api_key:'k',key_id:'kid',user_id:'uid'}); mockServer.handle('DELETE','/api/v1/keys/kid', 500, {error:'internal server error'}); result = runCLI(['keys','revoke']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'internal server error'; cfg = readParsedConfig(); assert cfg.api_key == 'k'; assert cfg.key_id == 'kid'

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc workspace create resolves team slug to UUID via GET /api/v1/teams, then POSTs the workspace with correct payload and prints pretty-printed JSON to stdout.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key='k'
Mock server returns [{slug:'my-team',id:'team-uuid'}] for GET /api/v1/teams
Mock server returns workspace JSON for POST /api/v1/workspaces
test_cases.input: afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo --slug my-ws --branch main --team my-team
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; POST /api/v1/workspaces body contains git_url, slug, branch, team_id='team-uuid'; stdout contains pretty-printed workspace JSON
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.handle('GET','/api/v1/teams', 200, [{slug:'my-team',id:'team-uuid'}]); mockServer.handle('POST','/api/v1/workspaces', 200, {id:'ws-1',slug:'my-ws'}); result = runCLI(['workspace','create','--git-url','https://github.com/org/repo','--slug','my-ws','--branch','main','--team','my-team']); assert result.exitCode == 0; body = jsonParse(mockServer.lastRequestBody('POST','/api/v1/workspaces')); assert body.git_url == 'https://github.com/org/repo'; assert body.slug == 'my-ws'; assert body.branch == 'main'; assert body.team_id == 'team-uuid'; parsed = jsonParse(result.stdout); assert parsed.slug == 'my-ws'

test_cases.description: Verifies that when team slug resolution returns zero matches, the CLI prints 'team not found: <slug>' and exits with code 1 without creating the workspace.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key
Mock server returns empty list [] for GET /api/v1/teams
test_cases.input: afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo --slug my-ws --team nonexistent-team
test_cases.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains 'team not found: nonexistent-team'; POST /api/v1/workspaces not called
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.handle('GET','/api/v1/teams', 200, []); result = runCLI(['workspace','create','--git-url','https://github.com/org/repo','--slug','my-ws','--team','nonexistent-team']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'team not found: nonexistent-team'; assert mockServer.notReceived('POST','/api/v1/workspaces')

test_cases.description: Verifies that when team slug resolution returns multiple matches, the CLI prints 'ambiguous team slug: <slug>' and exits with code 1 without creating the workspace.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key
Mock server returns two teams with slug='dup-team' for GET /api/v1/teams
test_cases.input: afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo --slug my-ws --team dup-team
test_cases.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains 'ambiguous team slug: dup-team'; POST /api/v1/workspaces not called
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.handle('GET','/api/v1/teams', 200, [{slug:'dup-team',id:'id1'},{slug:'dup-team',id:'id2'}]); result = runCLI(['workspace','create','--git-url','https://github.com/org/repo','--slug','my-ws','--team','dup-team']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'ambiguous team slug: dup-team'; assert mockServer.notReceived('POST','/api/v1/workspaces')

test_cases.description: Verifies that branch and team_id are omitted from the POST /api/v1/workspaces payload when --branch and --team are not provided.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key
Mock server captures POST /api/v1/workspaces request body
test_cases.input: afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo --slug my-ws (no --branch, no --team)
test_cases.expected: POST /api/v1/workspaces body contains only git_url and slug; branch and team_id keys are absent
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.handle('POST','/api/v1/workspaces', 200, {slug:'my-ws'}); result = runCLI(['workspace','create','--git-url','https://github.com/org/repo','--slug','my-ws']); body = jsonParse(mockServer.lastRequestBody('POST','/api/v1/workspaces')); assert 'branch' not in body; assert 'team_id' not in body; assert body.git_url == 'https://github.com/org/repo'

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc workspace list sends GET /api/v1/workspaces with Bearer auth and prints pretty-printed JSON to stdout.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key='k'
Mock server returns [{slug:'ws1'}] for GET /api/v1/workspaces
test_cases.input: afc workspace list
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; GET /api/v1/workspaces called with Authorization: Bearer k; stdout contains pretty-printed JSON array with 2-space indent
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.handle('GET','/api/v1/workspaces', 200, [{slug:'ws1'}]); result = runCLI(['workspace','list']); assert result.exitCode == 0; req = mockServer.lastRequest('GET','/api/v1/workspaces'); assert req.headers['Authorization'] == 'Bearer k'; assert result.stdout contains '  '; parsed = jsonParse(result.stdout); assert parsed[0].slug == 'ws1'

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc workspace get <slug> sends GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug with Bearer auth and prints pretty-printed JSON to stdout.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key='k'
Mock server returns {slug:'my-ws'} for GET /api/v1/workspaces/my-ws
test_cases.input: afc workspace get my-ws
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; GET /api/v1/workspaces/my-ws called with Bearer auth; stdout contains pretty-printed workspace JSON
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.handle('GET','/api/v1/workspaces/my-ws', 200, {slug:'my-ws',id:'ws-1'}); result = runCLI(['workspace','get','my-ws']); assert result.exitCode == 0; req = mockServer.lastRequest('GET','/api/v1/workspaces/my-ws'); assert req.headers['Authorization'] == 'Bearer k'; parsed = jsonParse(result.stdout); assert parsed.slug == 'my-ws'

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc workspace token create sends POST /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens with expires integer, prints the full token JSON to stdout, and does not persist the token to config.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key='k'
Mock server returns {id:'tok-1',token:'secret-val'} for POST /api/v1/workspaces/my-ws/tokens
test_cases.input: afc workspace token create --workspace my-ws --expires 30
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; stdout contains token JSON including secret-val; config.toml does not contain secret-val; POST body has expires=30 (integer)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.handle('POST','/api/v1/workspaces/my-ws/tokens', 200, {id:'tok-1',token:'secret-val'}); result = runCLI(['workspace','token','create','--workspace','my-ws','--expires','30']); assert result.exitCode == 0; parsed = jsonParse(result.stdout); assert parsed.token == 'secret-val'; configContents = readFile(configPath); assert configContents not_contains 'secret-val'; body = jsonParse(mockServer.lastRequestBody('POST','/api/v1/workspaces/my-ws/tokens')); assert typeof(body.expires) == integer; assert body.expires == 30

test_cases.description: Verifies that label is omitted from the workspace token create payload when --label is not provided, and expires is always included as an integer.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key
Mock server captures POST /api/v1/workspaces/ws1/tokens request
test_cases.input: afc workspace token create --workspace ws1 (no --label provided)
test_cases.expected: POST body has expires as integer; label key is absent from payload
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.handle('POST','/api/v1/workspaces/ws1/tokens', 200, {id:'t1',token:'s'}); runCLI(['workspace','token','create','--workspace','ws1']); body = jsonParse(mockServer.lastRequestBody('POST','/api/v1/workspaces/ws1/tokens')); assert 'label' not in body; assert typeof(body.expires) == integer

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc workspace token list sends GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens with Bearer auth and prints metadata JSON to stdout.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key='k'
Mock server returns [{id:'t1',label:'ci'}] for GET /api/v1/workspaces/ws1/tokens
test_cases.input: afc workspace token list --workspace ws1
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; GET /api/v1/workspaces/ws1/tokens called with Bearer auth; stdout contains pretty-printed token metadata JSON array
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.handle('GET','/api/v1/workspaces/ws1/tokens', 200, [{id:'t1',label:'ci'}]); result = runCLI(['workspace','token','list','--workspace','ws1']); assert result.exitCode == 0; req = mockServer.lastRequest('GET','/api/v1/workspaces/ws1/tokens'); assert req.headers['Authorization'] == 'Bearer k'; parsed = jsonParse(result.stdout); assert parsed[0].id == 't1'

test_cases.description: Verifies that afc workspace token revoke sends DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens/:token_id and prints 'Token <token-id> revoked.' to stderr on success.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key='k'
Mock server returns 204 for DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/ws1/tokens/tok-abc
test_cases.input: afc workspace token revoke --workspace ws1 tok-abc
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/ws1/tokens/tok-abc called; stderr contains 'Token tok-abc revoked.'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.handle('DELETE','/api/v1/workspaces/ws1/tokens/tok-abc', 204, ''); result = runCLI(['workspace','token','revoke','--workspace','ws1','tok-abc']); assert result.exitCode == 0; assert result.stderr contains 'Token tok-abc revoked.'; assert mockServer.received('DELETE','/api/v1/workspaces/ws1/tokens/tok-abc')

test_cases.description: Verifies that omitting the <token-id> positional argument for workspace token revoke causes Cobra's ExactArgs(1) to print a usage error and exit with code 1.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key
No network request expected
test_cases.input: afc workspace token revoke --workspace ws1 (no token-id argument)
test_cases.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains Cobra usage error about wrong number of arguments; no network request made
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: networkCalls=[]; result = runCLI(['workspace','token','revoke','--workspace','ws1'], mockHTTP(networkCalls)); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'accepts 1 arg' OR result.stderr contains 'argument'; assert len(networkCalls) == 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that all raw API response bodies are printed to stdout pretty-printed with 2-space indentation without any field reshaping.
preconditions: Mock server returns a specific JSON object for GET /api/v1/workspaces
Config file has hub_url, api_key
test_cases.input: afc workspace list; server returns [{"id":"w1","slug":"ws","git_url":"https://g.com"}]
test_cases.expected: stdout contains the JSON array with all original fields preserved and formatted with 2-space indentation; no fields added, removed, or renamed
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: serverResp = [{id:'w1',slug:'ws',git_url:'https://g.com'}]; mockServer.handle('GET','/api/v1/workspaces', 200, serverResp); result = runCLI(['workspace','list']); assert result.stdout == jsonIndent(serverResp, 2); parsed = jsonParse(result.stdout); assert parsed[0].id == 'w1'; assert parsed[0].git_url == 'https://g.com'

test_cases.description: Verifies that all human-readable status and error messages are written to stderr only, and stdout contains no human-readable messages.
preconditions: Mock server returns 204 for DELETE /api/v1/keys/kid
Config file has hub_url, api_key, key_id='kid'
test_cases.input: afc keys revoke (produces a status message 'API key revoked.')
test_cases.expected: stderr contains 'API key revoked.'; stdout is empty or contains only valid JSON; 'API key revoked.' does not appear in stdout
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: result = runCLI(['keys','revoke']); assert result.stderr contains 'API key revoked.'; assert result.stdout not_contains 'API key revoked.'; assert result.stdout == '' OR jsonParse(result.stdout) is valid

test_cases.description: Verifies that all authenticated API requests include the Authorization: Bearer <api_key> header.
preconditions: Config file has api_key='my-secret-key'
Mock server captures request headers
test_cases.input: afc keys list
test_cases.expected: Request to GET /api/v1/keys includes header Authorization: Bearer my-secret-key
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: writeConfig({api_key:'my-secret-key'}); mockServer.handle('GET','/api/v1/keys', 200, []); runCLI(['keys','list']); req = mockServer.lastRequest('GET','/api/v1/keys'); assert req.headers['Authorization'] == 'Bearer my-secret-key'

test_cases.description: Verifies that the HTTP client uses a fixed 30-second timeout for all outbound requests.
preconditions: Mock HTTP server is configured to delay response beyond 30 seconds (or test inspects the http.Client timeout field directly)
Config file has hub_url, api_key
test_cases.input: afc keys list against a mock server that delays > 30s
test_cases.expected: Request times out; exit code 1; stderr contains a timeout error message; OR http.Client.Timeout is verified to equal 30 seconds
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: client = buildHTTPClient(); assert client.Timeout == 30 * time.Second; // OR: mockServer.delayResponse(31 * time.Second); result = runCLI(['keys','list']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'timeout' OR result.stderr contains 'deadline'

test_cases.description: Verifies that a non-2xx response with a non-JSON body (e.g., HTML 502) causes the CLI to print the clean error message without the raw body and exit with code 1.
preconditions: Mock server returns 502 with HTML body '<html><body>Bad Gateway</body></html>' for any request
Config file has hub_url, api_key
test_cases.input: afc keys list; server returns 502 with HTML body
test_cases.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains 'Error: unexpected response from server (HTTP 502).'; raw HTML body not present in stderr or stdout
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.handle('GET','/api/v1/keys', 502, '<html><body>Bad Gateway</body></html>', contentType='text/html'); result = runCLI(['keys','list']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'Error: unexpected response from server (HTTP 502).'; assert result.stderr not_contains '<html>'; assert result.stdout not_contains '<html>'

property_tests.description: For any combination of flag, env var, and config file values, the resolved config value always comes from the highest-priority source that is non-empty.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate random string triples (flagVal, envVal, configVal) where any subset may be empty; enumerate all 8 combinations of present/absent for each source
property_tests.invariant_check: resolved = resolveConfigValue(flag=flagVal, env=envVal, file=configVal); if flagVal != '' { assert resolved == flagVal } else if envVal != '' { assert resolved == envVal } else if configVal != '' { assert resolved == configVal } else { assert error returned }

property_tests.description: For any config mutation operation, the config file is either fully updated or entirely unchanged — a process termination between temp write and rename never produces a corrupt intermediate state.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Simulate crashes (panic/kill) at random points during the atomic write; read the config file after recovery
property_tests.invariant_check: for each simulated crash point: configContents = readFile(configPath); assert (configContents == oldConfig OR configContents == newConfig); assert toml.Decode(configContents) succeeds (not corrupt)

property_tests.description: For any two separate invocations of afc login, the generated CSRF state parameters are distinct and each contains at least 16 bytes of cryptographically random entropy.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate N=1000 CSRF state values using the same generateState() function
property_tests.invariant_check: states = [generateState() for _ in range(1000)]; assert len(set(states)) == 1000 (all unique); for s in states: assert len(base64URLDecode(s)) >= 16

property_tests.description: For any invocation of afc workspace token create, the token secret value returned by the server never appears in $HOME/.af/config.toml.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate random token secret strings of varying lengths; mock the POST /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens server to return them
property_tests.invariant_check: for each tokenSecret in randomSecrets: runCLI(['workspace','token','create','--workspace','ws']); configContents = readFile(configPath); assert tokenSecret not in configContents

property_tests.description: For any afc command invocation, stdout contains only valid JSON or is empty, and stderr contains only human-readable strings; the two streams never exchange their content.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Invoke each CLI command (list, get, create, revoke, refresh, login) with both success and error mock responses
property_tests.invariant_check: for each command in allCommands: result = runCLI(command); if result.stdout != '': assert isValidJSON(result.stdout); assert result.stdout not_contains any(humanMsgs); assert result.stderr not_contains isValidTopLevelJSON(result.stderr)

property_tests.description: For any --expires value not in {0, 30, 60, 90}, the CLI exits with code 1 before issuing any HTTP request, for both afc login and afc workspace token create.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate integer values: all integers in [-5, 120] not in {0, 30, 60, 90}, plus random large values, plus non-integer strings
property_tests.invariant_check: for each invalidExpires in invalidValues: networkCalls=[]; r1=runCLI(['login','--provider','github','--expires',str(invalidExpires)], captureNetwork(networkCalls)); assert r1.exitCode==1; assert len(networkCalls)==0; assert r1.stderr contains 'Error: --expires must be one of: 0, 30, 60, 90'; networkCalls=[]; r2=runCLI(['workspace','token','create','--workspace','ws','--expires',str(invalidExpires)], captureNetwork(networkCalls)); assert r2.exitCode==1; assert len(networkCalls)==0

property_tests.description: For any non-2xx HTTP response whose body is not parseable as JSON, the CLI never prints the raw response body to stderr or stdout.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary non-JSON response bodies (HTML, binary, plain text, empty) with various non-2xx status codes (400, 403, 404, 500, 502, 503)
property_tests.invariant_check: for each (statusCode, body) in nonJSONResponses: mockServer.respond(statusCode, body, contentType='text/html'); result = runCLI(anyCommand); assert result.stderr not_contains body; assert result.stdout not_contains body; assert result.stderr contains 'Error: unexpected response from server (HTTP '+str(statusCode)+').'

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when the $HOME/.af/ directory cannot be created due to permissions, the CLI prints a descriptive error and exits with code 1.
preconditions: Test uses a temp directory as $HOME where the parent directory is read-only (mode 0555)
afc binary is built and available
edge_case_tests.input: Any afc command invoked in an environment where $HOME/.af/ cannot be created
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains a descriptive filesystem error; no partial state (no partial directory or file) left
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: tmpHome = createTempDir(); chmod(tmpHome, 0555); setEnv('HOME', tmpHome); result = runCLI(['keys','list']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'Error' OR result.stderr contains 'permission'; assert not exists(tmpHome + '/.af')

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when $HOME/.af/config.toml exists as a directory rather than a file, the CLI prints a descriptive error and exits with code 1.
preconditions: A directory named config.toml exists at $HOME/.af/config.toml
Test uses a temp directory as $HOME
edge_case_tests.input: Any afc command invoked when $HOME/.af/config.toml is a directory
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains a descriptive error about the config path; no crash or panic
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: tmpHome = createTempDir(); mkdirAll(tmpHome + '/.af/config.toml'); result = runCLI(['keys','list']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'Error' OR result.stderr contains 'config'

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when both AF_HUB_URL env var and hub_url config file field are set (and --hub-url is not provided), the env var value is used.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url='config-url'
AF_HUB_URL env var is set to 'env-url'
--hub-url flag not provided
edge_case_tests.input: afc workspace list with AF_HUB_URL='env-url' and config hub_url='config-url'
edge_case_tests.expected: Effective hub_url used for the HTTP request is 'env-url'; 'config-url' is not used
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: writeConfig({hub_url:'config-url',api_key:'k'}); setEnv('AF_HUB_URL','env-url'); mockEnvServer = startServerAt('env-url'); result = runCLI(['workspace','list']); assert mockEnvServer.received('GET','/api/v1/workspaces')

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when --hub-url flag is provided alongside AF_HUB_URL env var and a config file value, the flag value takes highest precedence.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url='config-url'
AF_HUB_URL='env-url' is set
--hub-url flag is provided with 'flag-url'
edge_case_tests.input: afc workspace list --hub-url flag-url
edge_case_tests.expected: Effective hub_url is 'flag-url'; requests go to the flag-url server
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: writeConfig({hub_url:'config-url',api_key:'k'}); setEnv('AF_HUB_URL','env-url'); mockFlagServer = startServerAt('flag-url'); result = runCLI(['workspace','list','--hub-url','flag-url']); assert mockFlagServer.received('GET','/api/v1/workspaces')

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when os.CreateTemp fails during an atomic config write, the CLI prints a descriptive error and leaves the original config unchanged.
preconditions: os.CreateTemp is stubbed to return an error
Existing config file with known content
edge_case_tests.input: Config mutation operation (e.g., keys refresh) when os.CreateTemp returns an error
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains a descriptive error about temp file creation; original config.toml is unchanged
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: originalContent = readFile(configPath); stubCreateTempError(error('no space left on device')); result = runConfigMutation(); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'Error' OR result.stderr contains 'temp'; assert readFile(configPath) == originalContent

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when os.Rename fails after temp file is written, the CLI prints a descriptive error, leaves original config unchanged, and the temp file may remain.
preconditions: os.Rename is stubbed to return an error after temp file is written
Existing config file with known content
edge_case_tests.input: Config mutation when os.Rename fails
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains descriptive error about rename; original config.toml unchanged; temp file may remain in $HOME/.af/
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: originalContent = readFile(configPath); stubRenameError(error('cross-device link')); result = runConfigMutation(); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'Error'; assert readFile(configPath) == originalContent

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that format, URL validity, slug character, and label length validation for workspace create fields are deferred to the server, and the server error response is forwarded.
preconditions: Mock server returns 422 with {error:'invalid git URL format'} for POST /api/v1/workspaces
Config file has hub_url, api_key
edge_case_tests.input: afc workspace create --git-url 'not-a-url' --slug 'INVALID SLUG!'
edge_case_tests.expected: CLI sends the request to the server without local format validation; exit code 1; stderr contains 'invalid git URL format' from server response
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.handle('POST','/api/v1/workspaces', 422, {error:'invalid git URL format'}); result = runCLI(['workspace','create','--git-url','not-a-url','--slug','INVALID SLUG!']); assert mockServer.received('POST','/api/v1/workspaces'); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'invalid git URL format'

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when the OAuth callback 2-minute timeout elapses, the CLI shuts down the callback server, prints a timeout error to stderr, and exits with code 1 without modifying config.
preconditions: Mock server returns providers list
Callback timeout is reduced to a short duration for testing (e.g., via test config override)
Config file with hub_url; no callback is sent to the local server
edge_case_tests.input: afc login --provider github; no browser callback within timeout period
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains a timeout/deadline error message; config.toml is unchanged
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: originalConfig = readFile(configPath); result = runCLIWithTimeout(loginCmd, waitDuration=testTimeout+1s); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'timeout' OR result.stderr contains 'deadline' OR result.stderr contains '2 minutes'; assert readFile(configPath) == originalConfig

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when browser.OpenURL fails, the CLI continues the login flow and the authorization URL is still printed to stderr.
preconditions: browser.OpenURL stub returns an error
Mock server returns providers list
Login flow is in progress
edge_case_tests.input: afc login --provider github; browser.OpenURL returns error
edge_case_tests.expected: Login flow continues waiting for callback; authorization URL appears on stderr; no error exit at this point
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: stubBrowserOpen(error('exec not found')); authURLs = []; result = runLoginAsync(['--provider','github']); assert result.stderr contains 'http://localhost:'; assert result.stderr contains '/callback'; assert loginFlowIsWaitingForCallback()

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that an OAuth callback with a mismatched CSRF state is ignored, an error is printed to stderr, and the CLI exits with code 1 without storing credentials.
preconditions: Login flow is in progress waiting for callback
Config file initially has no credentials
edge_case_tests.input: Callback received at /callback?code=valid-code&state=WRONG-STATE
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains an error about invalid/mismatched state; config.toml unchanged (no credentials written)
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: originalConfig = readFile(configPath); sendCallback(code='valid-code', state='WRONG-STATE'); result = waitForLogin(); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'state' OR result.stderr contains 'invalid'; assert readParsedConfig().api_key == ''

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when the local callback server port binding fails, the CLI prints a descriptive error and exits with code 1 before opening the browser.
preconditions: OS port binding is stubbed to fail
browser.OpenURL stub tracks calls
edge_case_tests.input: afc login --provider github; net.Listen returns an error
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains a descriptive error; browser.OpenURL is never called
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: browserCalled = false; stubBrowserOpen(fn(){ browserCalled = true }); stubListenError(error('bind failed')); result = runCLI(['login','--provider','github']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'Error'; assert browserCalled == false

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when GET /api/v1/auth/providers returns a network error or non-2xx, the CLI exits with code 1 before initiating any OAuth flow.
preconditions: Mock server returns 503 for GET /api/v1/auth/providers
browser.OpenURL stub tracks calls
edge_case_tests.input: afc login --provider github; providers endpoint returns 503
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains an error about the providers endpoint; browser not opened; no callback server started
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: browserCalled = false; stubBrowserOpen(fn(){ browserCalled = true }); mockServer.handle('GET','/api/v1/auth/providers', 503, {error:'service unavailable'}); result = runCLI(['login','--provider','github']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert browserCalled == false; assert result.stderr contains 'Error'

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a non-2xx response from GET /api/v1/keys causes the CLI to print the JSON error message or the non-JSON error message and exit with code 1.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key
Mock server returns 401 with {error: 'unauthorized'} for GET /api/v1/keys
edge_case_tests.input: afc keys list; server returns 401 with JSON error
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains 'unauthorized'; stdout is empty
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.handle('GET','/api/v1/keys', 401, {error:'unauthorized'}); result = runCLI(['keys','list']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'unauthorized'; assert result.stdout == ''

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a non-2xx response from POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh causes the CLI to print the error and exit with code 1 without modifying config.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key='old', key_id='kid'
Mock server returns 403 with {error:'forbidden'} for POST /api/v1/keys/kid/refresh
edge_case_tests.input: afc keys refresh; server returns 403
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains 'forbidden'; config unchanged (api_key still 'old')
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: writeConfig({api_key:'old',key_id:'kid'}); mockServer.handle('POST','/api/v1/keys/kid/refresh', 403, {error:'forbidden'}); result = runCLI(['keys','refresh']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'forbidden'; assert readParsedConfig().api_key == 'old'

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when atomic config write fails after a successful server revocation, the CLI prints a warning and exits with code 1.
preconditions: Mock server returns 204 for DELETE /api/v1/keys/kid
os.Rename is stubbed to fail after the server responds with 2xx
edge_case_tests.input: afc keys revoke; server revokes key successfully; atomic write fails
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains a descriptive error about the config write failure; config may not be cleared (stale values may remain)
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: writeConfig({api_key:'k',key_id:'kid',user_id:'uid'}); mockServer.handle('DELETE','/api/v1/keys/kid', 204, ''); stubRenameError(error('disk full')); result = runCLI(['keys','revoke']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'Error'

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a network error during GET /api/v1/teams for team slug resolution causes the CLI to print a descriptive error and exit with code 1 without sending the workspace creation request.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key
Mock server is unreachable or returns a connection error for GET /api/v1/teams
edge_case_tests.input: afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo --slug ws --team my-team; teams request fails with network error
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains a descriptive network error; POST /api/v1/workspaces not called
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.rejectConnection('/api/v1/teams'); result = runCLI(['workspace','create','--git-url','https://github.com/org/repo','--slug','ws','--team','my-team']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'Error'; assert mockServer.notReceived('POST','/api/v1/workspaces')

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a non-2xx non-JSON response from POST /api/v1/workspaces (e.g., HTML 502) produces the clean error message without raw HTML.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key
Mock server returns 502 with HTML body for POST /api/v1/workspaces
edge_case_tests.input: afc workspace create --git-url https://g.com/r --slug ws; server returns 502 HTML
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains 'Error: unexpected response from server (HTTP 502).'; raw HTML not in stderr or stdout
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.handle('GET','/api/v1/teams', 200, []); mockServer.handle('POST','/api/v1/workspaces', 502, '<html>Bad Gateway</html>', contentType='text/html'); result = runCLI(['workspace','create','--git-url','https://g.com/r','--slug','ws']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'Error: unexpected response from server (HTTP 502).'; assert result.stderr not_contains '<html>'

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a 404 response from GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug causes the CLI to print the JSON error message and exit with code 1.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key
Mock server returns 404 with {error:'workspace not found'} for GET /api/v1/workspaces/no-such-ws
edge_case_tests.input: afc workspace get no-such-ws
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains 'workspace not found'; stdout is empty
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.handle('GET','/api/v1/workspaces/no-such-ws', 404, {error:'workspace not found'}); result = runCLI(['workspace','get','no-such-ws']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'workspace not found'; assert result.stdout == ''

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a non-2xx response from POST /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens causes the CLI to print the error and exit with code 1 without storing anything in config.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key
Mock server returns 403 with {error:'forbidden'} for POST /api/v1/workspaces/ws1/tokens
edge_case_tests.input: afc workspace token create --workspace ws1 --expires 30; server returns 403
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains 'forbidden'; config unchanged; no token stored
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.handle('POST','/api/v1/workspaces/ws1/tokens', 403, {error:'forbidden'}); result = runCLI(['workspace','token','create','--workspace','ws1','--expires','30']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'forbidden'; configContents = readFile(configPath); assert configContents not_contains 'token'

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a non-2xx response from DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens/:token_id causes the CLI to print the error and exit with code 1.
preconditions: Config file has hub_url, api_key
Mock server returns 404 with {error:'token not found'} for DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/ws1/tokens/t1
edge_case_tests.input: afc workspace token revoke --workspace ws1 t1; server returns 404
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains 'token not found'; stdout is empty
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.handle('DELETE','/api/v1/workspaces/ws1/tokens/t1', 404, {error:'token not found'}); result = runCLI(['workspace','token','revoke','--workspace','ws1','t1']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'token not found'

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when an outbound HTTP request times out (exceeds 30 seconds), the CLI prints a timeout error to stderr and exits with code 1 without retrying.
preconditions: HTTP client timeout is set to 30 seconds
Mock server is configured to hang indefinitely
edge_case_tests.input: afc keys list against a server that never responds
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains a timeout/deadline error; no retry attempt made
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mockServer.hangForever('/api/v1/keys'); result = runCLIWithShortTimeout(['keys','list'], clientTimeout=100ms); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'timeout' OR result.stderr contains 'deadline'; assert mockServer.requestCount('/api/v1/keys') == 1

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a connection refused network error causes the CLI to print a descriptive error and exit with code 1 without retrying.
preconditions: hub_url points to a port where nothing is listening
Config file has hub_url, api_key
edge_case_tests.input: afc keys list with hub_url pointing to a non-listening port
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code 1; stderr contains a descriptive network error (connection refused); no retry; stdout is empty
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: writeConfig({hub_url:'http://localhost:1',api_key:'k'}); result = runCLI(['keys','list']); assert result.exitCode == 1; assert result.stderr contains 'connection refused' OR result.stderr contains 'Error'; assert result.stdout == ''

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test: operator logs in via OAuth and then creates a workspace with team association, verifying config is written and workspace JSON is printed.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run 'afc login --provider github --expires 90' followed by 'afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo --slug my-workspace --team my-team'
mockable: browser.OpenURL stub (captures auth URL, triggers callback automatically)
expected_effects: Config file $HOME/.af/config.toml written with hub_url, user_id='u-1', api_key='secret', key_id='k-1'
Authorization URL printed to stderr during login
POST /api/v1/auth/callback called with provider='github', expires=90, redirect_uri matching http://localhost:<port>/callback
GET /api/v1/teams called with Authorization: Bearer secret
POST /api/v1/workspaces payload contains git_url, slug='my-workspace', team_id='team-uuid'
stdout contains pretty-printed workspace JSON with slug='my-workspace'
Both commands exit with code 0

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for the keys refresh happy path: operator refreshes their API key and the config is updated with new credentials.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run 'afc keys refresh' after config is populated with hub_url, api_key='old-key', key_id='old-kid'
expected_effects: POST /api/v1/keys/old-kid/refresh called with Authorization: Bearer old-key
Config file updated with api_key='new-key', key_id='new-kid'
stdout contains pretty-printed new key JSON with id='new-kid'
Exit code 0

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for OAuth callback timeout: operator starts login but does not complete browser flow; CLI exits with error after timeout.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run 'afc login --provider github' with a reduced callback timeout; do not send any callback to the local server
mockable: browser.OpenURL stub (captures URL, does not trigger callback)
Callback timeout duration (reduced for test speed)
expected_effects: Authorization URL printed to stderr
CLI waits for callback until timeout fires
Callback server is shut down after timeout
stderr contains a timeout error message
Exit code 1
Config file unchanged (no credentials written)

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for workspace token revoke: operator revokes a specific token and receives confirmation on stderr.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run 'afc workspace token revoke --workspace my-workspace abc-token-id'
expected_effects: DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/my-workspace/tokens/abc-token-id called with Authorization: Bearer <api_key>
stderr contains 'Token abc-token-id revoked.'
stdout is empty
Exit code 0

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for keys revoke with 404 (already revoked): operator revokes a key that is already gone from the server; local credentials are still cleared.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run 'afc keys revoke' with config containing api_key='gone-key', key_id='gone-kid', user_id='gone-uid'
expected_effects: DELETE /api/v1/keys/gone-kid called with Authorization: Bearer gone-key
Config api_key cleared to empty string
Config key_id cleared to empty string
Config user_id cleared to empty string
stderr contains 'API key not found on server. Local credentials cleared.'
Exit code 0
